title: Error when using a for loop variable in a slice range
body:
Using the latest git build. I attached the SystemVerilog code causing the issue.

[loop_variable_in_range.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/17259786/loop_variable_in_range.txt)

I would expect `for` loop variables to be seen as constants within each loop iteration, instead I get an error. The code compiles well in Questa (I only tried with the original code this example was made from). This is the Verilator output:

```
%Error: loop_variable_in_range.sv:37:32: Expecting expression to be constant, but variable isn't const: 'i'
                                       : ... note: In instance 'loop_variable_in_range'
   37 |             thr_b[i] = |pry[0+:i+1];
      |                                ^
%Error: loop_variable_in_range.sv:37:33: Width of :+ or :- bit slice range isn't a constant
                                       : ... note: In instance 'loop_variable_in_range'
   37 |             thr_b[i] = |pry[0+:i+1];
      |                                 ^
%Error: loop_variable_in_range.sv:41:29: First value of [a:b] isn't a constant, maybe you want +: or -:
                                       : ... note: In instance 'loop_variable_in_range'
   41 |             thr_c[i] = |pry[i:0];
      |                             ^
```
code_blocks:
%Error: loop_variable_in_range.sv:37:32: Expecting expression to be constant, but variable isn't const: 'i'
                                       : ... note: In instance 'loop_variable_in_range'
   37 |             thr_b[i] = |pry[0+:i+1];
      |                                ^
%Error: loop_variable_in_range.sv:37:33: Width of :+ or :- bit slice range isn't a constant
                                       : ... note: In instance 'loop_variable_in_range'
   37 |             thr_b[i] = |pry[0+:i+1];
      |                                 ^
%Error: loop_variable_in_range.sv:41:29: First value of [a:b] isn't a constant, maybe you want +: or -:
                                       : ... note: In instance 'loop_variable_in_range'
   41 |             thr_c[i] = |pry[i:0];
      |                             ^
